title: The Lie Every Real Estate Office Tells New Agents
dek: Walk into almost any brokerage in America as a new agent and you'll hear a version of the same speech. Focus on sales. Rentals are fine for practice, but the real money is in closings. Be patient.
heroAlt: The Lie Every Real Estate Office Tells New Agents

blocks.html: It's delivered with total confidence, usually by someone who genuinely believes it. And it quietly wrecks more first-year careers than any market downturn.

The people repeating it aren't villains. They're echoes. Sales-first is the industry's oldest hand-me-down, passed from manager to recruit for generations, and almost nobody stops to check the math for a modern new agent's situation. The trainers teaching you never built a rental business, so they can't hand you a playbook they never owned.

blocks.text: You can't be handed a playbook your coach never owned.

blocks.html: The lie survives on status. Sales gets the awards, the TV shows, the champagne photos. Nobody posts a lease signing. So the picture of success every new agent carries is a sold sign, and rentals get filed under "beneath me" before anyone runs a single number.

Here's the number worth running: a lease pays roughly one month's rent and closes in days. A luxury lease in a major market can be a several-thousand-dollar commission event, repeatable multiple times per month, with no financing to survive and no escrow to wait out. Meanwhile the "real money" in sales arrives, for a typical new agent, half a year after they start needing it.

The truth isn't that sales money is fake. It's real, and for some agents at some stages, it's the right focus. The lie is the word "practice." Rentals aren't practice. They're a parallel business with a faster clock, a compounding client base, and almost no serious competition, precisely because everyone was told to ignore them.
When an entire industry agrees something is beneath it, the people who take that thing seriously get an open field.

blocks.text: Every market has agents starving next to money they were told not to touch.

blocks.html: I wrote the playbook the offices don't hand out. It's free: Luxury Leases, Real Profits. Better agents win.
